MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment On Behalf Of Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Continued To Mar-20-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Suzanne C. Frias, Esq. of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, T:415.690.2700, for defendant/moving party;
Mark J. Zanobini, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF MARK J. ZANOBINI PC, T:415.392.8400, for plaintiff, on Court Call.

RULING: Defendant's counsel contested and argued the tentative ruling. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, as follows:

Defendant Regents of the University of California's summary judgment motion is continued to March 20, 2020; plaintiff Anne-Marie Weber's CCP 437c(h) request is granted. Defendant's motion was filed July 22, 2019 and the hearing date has already been continued twice. This third continuance shall be the final one; the trial date of September 21, 2020 is maintained. Plaintiff's opposition shall be filed and served (including courtesy copies to this department) by March 6. Any further reply by defendant is due by March 13.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment On Behalf Of Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Continued To Dec-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Deem Requests For Admission, Set One, Admitted; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Suzanne Frias, Esq. 415-288-9800 of Hassard Bonnigton for Defendant The Regents of the University of California.
No Other Appearances.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Defendant's Counsel requests to specify a date by which Plaintiff is ordered to pay the awarded sanctions to Defendant sent by email. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's Motion To Deem Requests For Admission, Set One, Admitted; Request For Sanctions.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is rejected in part and adopted in part. Defendant's motion to deem requests for admission, set one, admitted is denied. Plaintiff served, before the hearing on the motion, a proposed response to the requests for admission (including a verification) that is in substantial compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220. (Code Civ. Proc. 2033.280(c); see St. Mary v. Superior Court (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 762, 776 ["a responding party's service, prior to the hearing on the 'deemed admitted' motion, of substantially compliant responses, will defeat a propounding party's attempt under section 2033.280 to have the RFAs deemed admitted"].) If defendant believes that the responses to RFAs and accompanying responses to form interrogatories are deficient in some respect, it may make a motion to compel further responses. (Id.) Because plaintiff's failure to serve a timely response to the requests for admission necessitated defendant's motion, plaintiff is ordered to pay defendant $1,267.50 in sanctions. (Code Civ. Proc. 2033.280(c).)

In addition, Plaintiff is ordered to pay defendant $1,267.50 in sanctions within 20 days of notice of entry of order. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery Requests, Request For Sanctions.

Defendant's unopposed motion is granted as follows: Within 28 days of service of notice of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall provide code compliant responses to Defendant's first sets of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production, dated February 6, 2019, and pay Defendant discovery sanctions in the amount of $858.00 (4.4 hours at $195/hour).
